title: Competency Inventory and Independent Evidence Ledger · Cumulative Campaign Review
summary: A delayed hiring review reconstructs competency inventory and independent evidence ledger from target, claim, evidence, audience, action, observation, changed case, rejected failure, repair, consent, owner, and next-decision evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for competency-to-evidence ledger after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Translate course, work, community, care, creative, and self-directed experience into observable role-relevant behaviors.
Rank claim evidence by authenticity, recency, independence, changed-case performance, stakeholder relevance, and inspectability.
Distinguish personal decisions and work from collaboration, templates, tutorials, automation, generative assistance, and reviewer input.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# job-search cumulative learner-controlled career campaign
# Browser labs review fictional evidence contracts only. They never contact people,
# submit applications, access accounts, expose personal data, negotiate, or accept offers.
workspace: career
candidate-id: fictional-learner
target-role: [replace with role family, work outcomes, and bounded level]
jurisdiction: [replace with location and current official guidance boundary]
audience: [replace with reviewer need, time, format, and access context]
claim: [replace with one truthful role-relevant behavior claim]
evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, observation, changed case, contribution, and limit]
process-state: [replace with discover, investigate, apply, interview, offer, close, or archive]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
privacy-consent: [replace with purpose, minimization, permission, retention, deletion, and safe contact]
decision: [replace with learner-owned next decision and stop rule]
transfer-boundary: learner-approved external actions only
review:

steps.title: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Competency Behavior
steps.instruction:
Order the Competency Behavior evidence cycle for this review task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Competency Behavior.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Competency Behavior assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Competency Behavior, keep Competency Behavior connected to mechanically reviewable target-role, truthful-claim, contribution, accessibility, privacy, consent, process-state, changed-candidate, failure, repair, decision, and transfer evidence.

content.text: A course-rich skills list cannot identify one independently reproduced behavior, changed case, personal contribution, current artifact, or honest limitation. The review variation 4 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Translate course, work, community, care, creative, and self-directed experience into observable role-relevant behaviors.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Competency Behavior has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: For Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Competency Behavior, name the learner goal, target role, jurisdiction, audience, truthful claim, contribution, source date, evidence revision, accessibility, privacy, consent, process state, decision owner, non-claim, and transfer boundary, Competency Behavior precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Competency Behavior, trace this concrete input and predict targeting, source, evidence, contribution, access, privacy, consent, process, decision, rejection, repair, follow-up, and learner-controlled transfer evidence before reviewing it: A course-rich skills list cannot identify one independently reproduced behavior, changed case, personal contribution, current artifact, or honest limitation. The review variation 4 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Translate course, work, community, care, creative, and self-directed experience into observable role-relevant behaviors.

For Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Competency Behavior, challenge the documented misconception with changed-candidate, target-role, source-date, jurisdiction, audience, access, privacy, consent, process-state, interview, offer, rejection, repair, and learner-controlled transfer evidence: Only paid employment produces valid professional evidence.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints for the review Competency Behavior case
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure for the review Competency Behavior case
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence for the review Competency Behavior case
Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise for the review Competency Behavior case

stimulus.title: Evidence Refresh case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A course-rich skills list cannot identify one independently reproduced behavior, changed case, personal contribution, current artifact, or honest limitation. The review variation 5 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Reproduce fragile or stale evidence under a changed requirement and record exact pass, failure, repair, and remaining limit.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: Old screenshots remain current evidence if the repository still exists.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Reproduce fragile or stale evidence under a changed requirement and record exact pass, failure, repair, and remaining limit. Artifact under review: competency-to-evidence ledger.

options.text: Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Evidence Refresh, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim fails the evidence boundary for Evidence Refresh.

steps.title: Record what required effort · Review Evidence Refresh
steps.instruction:
Write the review evidence note for Evidence Refresh: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A course-rich skills list cannot identify one independently reproduced behavior, changed case, personal contribution, current artifact, or honest limitation. The review variation 6 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Reproduce fragile or stale evidence under a changed requirement and record exact pass, failure, repair, and remaining limit.

Evidence target: Record what required effort · Review Evidence Refresh.

For Record what required effort · Review Evidence Refresh, keep Evidence Refresh connected to mechanically reviewable target-role, truthful-claim, contribution, accessibility, privacy, consent, process-state, changed-candidate, failure, repair, decision, and transfer evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Reproduce fragile or stale evidence under a changed requirement and record exact pass, failure, repair, and remaining limit.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.

checks.description: The decision applies Competency Behavior to the changed stakeholder case.
checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Competency Behavior.

checks.description: The decision applies Evidence Strength to the changed stakeholder case.
checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Evidence Strength.

checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Contribution Boundary.

checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Transfer Story.

checks.description: The decision applies Evidence Refresh to the changed stakeholder case.
checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Evidence Refresh.

checks.description: The decision applies Evidence Refresh to the changed stakeholder case.
checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Evidence Refresh.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using reproduce and fragile plus specific case evidence.
